metadata.title:
CARMAN THROWS LIGHT


ON ". ". P. METHODS

pages.raw_text:
Cali the wrecks that have occur
red on the lines of the Washington
Water Power Co. during the past
year are due directly or indirectly
to inexperienced men," said an old
employee of the company today. ut
Is the policy of the local monopoly
to discharge their old employees TO,
trivial attractions of the rules and
in this way the company is enabled
to keep down expenses, as the older
man grows in the service the
more money he is supposed to earn.
The places of the old employees are
filled with cheaper inexperienced
men, many of whom are 'floaters'
here to look the country over.


9TH statement of the dead
motorman, Nickerson," said the old
employee, may have been exception
al, as 1t Is not unusual tor the
brakes on the cars of the company
to be out of order. If you don't be-
lieve this statement, ask some Of
the other men. The air pipes con-
trolling the brakes frequently freeze
up. Mine have to be thawed out
nearly every trip during the cold
snap, and you can see men with
blow pipes engaged in thawing pipes
all day at Riverside and Howard,
This might have been the cause Of
the accident on the Minnehaha line.


1.-THE action of the company in
burning up the wreck is evidence
that they were onto the fact that
the brakes were faulty and they
wanted to destroy all evidence that
would substantiate the claims of
the dead motorman. The statement
of the conductor is not entitled to
much consideration, as he was not
in a position to know what the con-
dition of the brakes really was.


I'VE often find our cars In bad
condition after we take them out
and IL we refuse to run the car we
are generally reprimanded and Oft
entimes laid off as a punishment.
| have no doubt but that Nickerson
found the rails slippery and at
tempted to use sand. The sand fur
nished is of such a poor quality
that 1t packs and will not run
freely and it is not unusual for the
pipes to be out of line with the rail
several inches so we cannot get the
sand on the track even II 1t does
run.


Another thing that detracts
from the service Is the fact that
the men become discouraged. II
you had to work 10 or more hours
every day. and Sunday included, and
could only earn from 81 to $3.50 per
day at that, you would not grow
enthusiastic over your job but
would soon come to realize that
common labor Is much better than
running a car for the local monop.
oily, as you call It." =
